questions.question: When is p ÔåÆ q false?
questions.answer: p ÔåÆ q (p implies q) is FALSE only when p is TRUE and q is FALSE. In all other cases (TT, FT, FF), it's TRUE. Think of it as a promise: 'If it rains, I'll bring umbrella.' Promise broken only if it rains (T) but I don't bring umbrella (F).

questions.question: Prove p ÔåÆ q Ôëí ┬¼p Ôê¿ q using truth table.
questions.answer: Both have same truth values: TTÔåÆT=T,┬¼TÔê¿T=T | TFÔåÆF=F,┬¼TÔê¿F=F | FTÔåÆT=T,┬¼FÔê¿T=T | FFÔåÆT=T,┬¼FÔê¿F=T. All rows match, so they're equivalent.
